title: The Korea Herald covers Samsung and SK hynix pushing new memory layers for AI
description: Recent reports from The Korea Herald and other sources highlight developments in memory technology by Samsung and SK hynix as AI demands grow.

sources.title: Samsung, SK hynix push new memory layer as AI outgrows HBM alone
Token Usage to Surge 24-Fold by 2030, Making CXL Next AI Chip Battleground After HBM
Samsung, SK Hynix Race to Mass-Produce CXL 3.2 Memory This Year, Ushering in the 'Next Memory' Era

sections.heading: New Memory Layer Developments
paragraphs: According to The Korea Herald, Samsung and SK hynix are pushing a new memory layer as artificial intelligence outgrows HBM alone.

sections.heading: Future Projections
paragraphs: Seoul Economic Daily reports that token usage is expected to surge 24-fold by 2030. This projection suggests that CXL will become the next battleground for AI chips following HBM.

sections.heading: Mass Production Goals
paragraphs: BigGo Finance states that Samsung and SK Hynix are racing to mass-produce CXL 3.2 memory this year. This effort is described as ushering in the 'Next Memory' era.

faq.q: What did The Korea Herald report regarding Samsung and SK hynix?
faq.a: According to The Korea Herald, the companies are pushing a new memory layer because AI is outgrowing HBM alone.

faq.q: What is the forecast for token usage according to Seoul Economic Daily?
faq.a: Seoul Economic Daily reports that token usage is projected to surge 24-fold by 2030, positioning CXL as the next AI chip battleground.
